title_en: Scholarly Assessment and How to Use the Mustadrak
title_ar: التقييم العلمي وكيفية الاستفادة من المستدرك
content_en:
The scholarly assessment of the Mustadrak has remained remarkably consistent across the centuries since its composition. Scholars universally acknowledge its importance as a repository of traditions not found in the primary Sahihs while simultaneously insisting on the necessity of using ad-Dhahabi's Talkhis alongside it. This paired reading — al-Hakim's original text together with ad-Dhahabi's critical assessment — has become the standard scholarly approach to the collection, and it is in this form that the Mustadrak is typically published and studied today.

Ad-Dhahabi's own complex legacy adds an additional layer to the scholarly conversation about the Mustadrak. Some scholars have noted that ad-Dhahabi's assessments in the Talkhis were themselves sometimes inconsistent or hasty, and that in some cases he confirmed as authentic traditions that other critics considered weak, or vice versa. This means that using the Mustadrak responsibly requires consulting not only ad-Dhahabi's review but also the broader hadith criticism literature, including Ibn Hajar al-Asqalani's relevant assessments in Fath al-Bari and Talkhis al-Habir.

For researchers working on specific legal or theological questions, the Mustadrak is most productively consulted as a source of additional chains for traditions already established through other routes, or as a source for traditions that may provide supplementary evidence for rulings. Hadith scholars in the Shafi'i tradition have made particularly extensive use of the Mustadrak, since al-Hakim himself was a Shafi'i and he often highlighted traditions with relevance to Shafi'i legal positions.

The modern scholar working with the Mustadrak has access to excellent scholarly editions with detailed indices and al-Arnaut's critical review added alongside al-Hakim's text and ad-Dhahabi's abridgment. Using the Mustadrak as a training ground in hadith criticism — practicing the evaluation of al-Hakim's authentication claims against the standard of later critics — is one of the most effective ways available to develop practical skills in the science of hadith evaluation.
